title: upload_blob: implement + test
description:
Why: Client needs to upload blobs to JMAP servers per RFC 8620 §6.1. The upload must verify SHA-256 if the server returns one (JMAP-CID capability).

What: Implement JmapChatClient::upload_blob in src/blob.rs:
  pub async fn upload_blob(&self, upload_url_template: &str, account_id: &str, data: &[u8], content_type: &str) -> Result<BlobUploadResponse, ClientError>

Algorithm:
1. Validate content_type via reqwest::header::HeaderValue::from_str, return InvalidHeaderValue on failure
2. Expand {accountId} in upload_url_template using expand_url_template
3. Build POST request: self.http.post(url).header(CONTENT_TYPE, content_type).body(data.to_vec())
4. Inject auth header from self.auth.auth_header() (pattern from fetch_session)
5. Send, call auth_failed_if(resp.status()), call error_for_status()
6. Deserialize response body as BlobUploadResponse
7. If sha256 is Some: validate it is exactly 64 lowercase hex chars (return Parse error if not); compute Sha256::digest(data), hex-encode, compare — return BlobIntegrityMismatch if mismatch
8. Test: wiremock POST /upload/account1/ returning upload_response.json fixture; assert blob_id and sha256 parsed correctly
9. Test: sha256 mismatch returns BlobIntegrityMismatch

Note: self.http and self.auth fields need pub(crate) visibility — change them in client.rs as part of this bead.

Defensive rules: no unwrap(); validate all inputs at entry point; do not log auth header or content bytes.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test passes; happy-path test and sha256-mismatch test both pass
